title: bus.tengine.gpu.heartbeat v1
description: When: Emitted every N frames (typically every 30 frames) by the GPU telemetry loop in a silo's render thread. Source: /tengine. Consumers use this to monitor GPU health, track FPS stability, and observe VRAM pressure over the lifetime of a session.

host.description: Hostname of the machine that generated this event.

session_id.description: Tengine session identifier, matching the session directory basename.

silo.description: Silo name this heartbeat originates from (e.g. 'Racing', 'Fluid').

frame.description: Current frame counter at time of this heartbeat.

fps.description: Measured frames per second over the sampling window.

vram_mb.description: Estimated GPU video memory usage in megabytes at time of heartbeat.

hottest_wts.description: Top work-types consuming GPU time in this frame, ordered by descending milliseconds.

ms.description: GPU time spent on this work-type in milliseconds for the current frame.
